page: Google Cloud Platform m4n-ultramem-56 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the m4n server family. It features 56 dedicated vCPUs and 1488.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a high memory-to-vCPU density of 26.57 GB per core.

This server configuration does not include local NVMe storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Due to the high memory density relative to the compute core count, this instance class is designed for memory-bound workloads rather than compute-heavy or graphics-accelerated tasks. It is qualitatively positioned as a high-resource-density option for large in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, and large-scale data analytics where massive datasets must reside entirely in system memory.
description: Google Cloud Platform m4n-ultramem-56 is an x86_64 architecture server within the m4n family, configured with 56 dedicated vCPUs and 1488.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a high resource density of 26.57 GB of memory per core, making it highly specialized for memory-intensive applications. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring external network-attached storage for data persistence. This hardware profile is designed for workloads that demand massive memory footprints relative to compute capacity, such as large in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy data analytics pipelines.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m4n-ultramem-56 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 56 dedicated vCPUs, 1488 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for large in-memory database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m4n-ultramem-56 is an x86_64 server with 56 dedicated vCPUs and 1488 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized configuration featuring 1488 GB of RAM and 56 dedicated vCPUs for large-scale in-memory enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: High memory-to-core ratio of 26.57 GB per vCPU
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Designed for in-memory databases and enterprise ERP systems
